behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff (diff extraction -> review-input builder). A unit test of one parser function can miss wiring mistakes where full-file blobs are passed through. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP do not apply: this can be validated with in-process fixtures. Rejected System because behavior text is about scoping logic, not real network/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run the diff-reader and input-construction modules together on fixture PR patches containing both changed and unchanged hunks; assert the generated review payload contains only changed-line context and excludes untouched file regions.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is GitHub diff-position semantics over real API calls. In-process mocks can accept incorrect position fields and hide failures (MOCK-HIDE). Requires real HTTP/API behavior, so System.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments using computed positions, then fetch review comments from GitHub and verify each appears on the intended changed line in the PR diff.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model judgment quality (detecting a real bug), which a stub cannot validate. This is the deterministic-assertion trap: wrapper logic can be tested with fakes, but defect identification quality cannot. Rejected Workflow because behavior asks for a single quality outcome (find >=1 real defect), not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Use a benchmark PR with a seeded known bug; run real model analysis and assert at least one returned finding correctly references that bug (or its direct symptom) in the changed code.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module state consistency (inline-post result IDs -> summary assembler). Unit tests of formatter logic alone miss handoff bugs. No required real OS/network behavior for the invariant itself; no model-output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute posting and summary-assembly modules with deterministic findings and stubbed comment-post responses containing run-scoped IDs; assert every summary entry references an ID created in that same run and no foreign/missing IDs exist.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when findings flow across modules (collector -> poster -> summary payload builder). A single-function unit test is insufficient for drop/merge bugs at boundaries. Rejected System because requirement is payload completeness before posting, testable in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline stages up to summary payload construction with a deterministic finding set containing multiple severities/paths; assert payload cardinality and IDs exactly match posted inline findings (no loss).

behaviors.description: The automation sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real pull request when provided a fixed precomputed finding set.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a real pull request and real sequence execution; real HTTP/API interactions are essential. Fixed findings remove LLM dependence, so Agentic is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the automation end-to-end on a real test PR using a fixed finding fixture; verify diff retrieval succeeds, inline comments are created, and summary comment posts successfully with expected references.

behaviors.description: The full live run with model-generated findings (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is a full agent pipeline with at least one real model-driven stage plus downstream handoff/posting stages; correctness depends on sequential orchestration of agentic + system steps. Rejected Agentic because behavior text requires end-to-end completion of the full sequence, not only model output quality in isolation.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the complete live pipeline on a real test PR with real model inference enabled; assert unattended completion and coherent artifacts across stages (findings produced, inline comments posted, final summary posted and consistent).
